summary: postinstall exfiltrates internal MySQL output to attacker-controlled OAST endpoint
details: package.json declares a postinstall lifecycle script that fires automatically on npm install. The script runs the mysql client against an internal RFC1918 host (10.108.193.167:3306) with hardcoded credentials, writes the query output to /data/mysql_result.txt, and POSTs the file's contents to a Burp Collaborator / oastify.com subdomain at http://3dhd6wwmusbh04m22igmzvb4hvnmblza.oastify.com. This causes any developer or CI runner that installs the package to (a) probe internal infrastructure and (b) exfiltrate command output to an attacker-controlled out-of-band host. The database name `supplychain_hunter` and the OAST destination shape are consistent with a dependency-confusion / supply-chain reconnaissance payload rather than a legitimate utility.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
